name: tfsec
description: Tfsec is a simple tool to detect potential security vulnerabilities in your terraformed infrastructure

options.description: Reduce the amount of output and no statistics
Config file to use during run
Explicitly the custom checks dir location
Enable debug logging (same as verbose)
Disable grouping of similar results
Provide comma-separated list of rule IDs to exclude from run
Remove results for downloaded modules in .terraform folder
Folder path to exclude, can be used multiple times and evaluated in order of specification
Filter results to return specific checks only (supports comma-delimited input)
Don't search for tf files, include everything below provided directory
Select output format: default, json, csv, checkstyle, junit, sarif. To use multiple formats, separate with a comma and specify a base output filename with --out. A file will be written for each type.
Help for tfsec
Stop and report an error if an HCL parse error is encountered
Include ignored checks in the result output
Include passed checks in the result output
Migrate ignore codes to the new ID structure
The minimum severity to report. One of CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW
Disable colored output (American style!)
Disable coloured output
Do not apply any ignore rules - normally ignored checks will fail
Do not download remote modules
Set output file. This filename will have a format descriptor appended if multiple formats are specified with --format
Print a JSON representation of the input supplied to rego policies
Directory to load rego policies from (recursively)
View statistics table of current findings
Run checks using a single thread
Runs checks but suppresses error code
Path to .tfvars file, can be used multiple times and evaluated in order of specification
Update to latest version
Enable verbose logging (same as debug)
Show version information and exit
Specify a workspace for ignore limits (default "default")
